featureName: BUG-014-002: rate-limit header parsing has no upper bound (chaos R30)

executionHistory.evidence: Parent-expanded chaos-hardening child workflow probe ran parseRetryAfter and updateRateLimits with adversarial float headers (NaN, +Inf, -Inf, Infinity, 86400 seconds, 99999999 seconds, 99999999999999.0 unix-seconds, max-float64). Four findings surfaced — F-CHAOS-R30-001 (MEDIUM: parseRetryAfter accepts NaN/Inf, falls through to float→time.Duration producing -2562047h47m16s), F-CHAOS-R30-002 (HIGH: parseRetryAfter has no upper bound — Retry-After: 86400 blocks sync goroutine 24h, 99999999 blocks 3+ years), F-CHAOS-R30-003 (MEDIUM: updateRateLimits accepts NaN/Inf X-RateLimit-Reset, poisons limiter state via int64(NaN)→MinInt64→year-1677 resetAt), F-CHAOS-R30-004 (HIGH: updateRateLimits has no upper bound on X-RateLimit-Reset — 99999999999999.0 makes ShouldWait return ~292,277 years; 9223372036854775000 overflows int64 silently disabling rate limiting). None had pre-existing coverage by C1-C4 or CHAOS-014-001..007 chaos batches.

Finding-owned planning: created BUG-014-002 folder with spec.md (HIGH+MEDIUM classification + 4-finding reproduction with concrete adversarial output), design.md (root cause for both non-finite-float and unbounded-finite-float axes + 4-part fix approach with ordering rationale — cap MUST be between finiteness guard and int64 conversion), scopes.md (single Scope 1 with 5 SCN-DC-FIX-002-NNN Gherkin scenarios + 6-row Test Plan + 7 DoD bullets each carrying scenario IDs and adversarial-fidelity evidence).

Modified internal/connector/discord/discord.go: added two named constants (maxRetryAfter = 5*time.Minute, maxRateLimitResetFromNow = 5*time.Minute) directly below maxErrorBodyExcerpt; hardened parseRetryAfter to reject NaN/Inf via math.IsNaN/math.IsInf and cap at maxRetryAfter BEFORE the float→time.Duration multiplication (avoids float-to-int overflow); hardened updateRateLimits to reject NaN/Inf X-RateLimit-Reset and cap resetFloat at (now + maxRateLimitResetFromNow) BEFORE the int64(resetFloat) conversion (avoids int64 overflow AND bounds awaitRateLimit wait). Function signatures unchanged. Boundary: discord.go is the only production file modified.

Added 6 adversarial regression tests to internal/connector/discord/discord_test.go: TestChaosR30_ParseRetryAfter_RejectsNonFiniteValues (6 sub-cases), TestChaosR30_ParseRetryAfter_CapsLargeValues (5 sub-cases), TestChaosR30_ParseRetryAfter_PreservesSmallValues (4 sub-cases), TestChaosR30_UpdateRateLimits_RejectsNonFiniteReset (4 sub-cases — hardened mid-round to assert limiter.buckets[route] non-presence, the only adversarial-fidelity-true check because ShouldWait==0 alone is coincidentally satisfied by int64(NaN)→MinInt64→year-1677→time.Until-negative→ShouldWait coerce-to-0), TestChaosR30_UpdateRateLimits_CapsAbsurdReset (4 sub-cases), TestChaosR30_UpdateRateLimits_PreservesLegitimateReset (1 case). `go test -race -count=1 -run 'TestChaosR30' ./internal/connector/discord/` → 6/6 tests PASS (24 sub-case PASS rows) ok 1.074s. Full package suite `go test -race -count=1 ./internal/connector/discord/` → ok 10.221s. Adversarial fidelity proof: temporarily reverted both production guards → 4 of 6 R30 tests FAIL with concrete diagnostics (parseRetryAfter("NaN") = -2562047h47m16s; parseRetryAfter("86400") = 24h0m0s; ShouldWait after absurd reset "99999999999999.0" = 2562047h47m16s ~292,277 years; limiter bucket for "/test/route" was created from NaN reset); restored guards → all 6 PASS again. Lesson learned and documented inline: tests MUST use make(http.Header) + h.Set(...) — map literals bypass http.Header key canonicalisation and silently make tests pass for the wrong reason. Transcript recorded in report.md.

All four F-CHAOS-R30-NNN findings have a fix and a regression test that demonstrably catches the bug if reverted (proven by revert+restore cycle captured in report.md). Rate-limit contract holds for both adversarial axes (non-finite floats AND absurd finite floats). No pre-existing discord-package test regressed: TestDoDiscordRequest_RateLimitHeaders, TestChaos_C1..C4, TestCHAOS_014_001..007, the gateway-poller tests, and the connector contract tests all PASS under race detector.

`go vet ./internal/connector/discord/...` exit=0 (clean). `gofmt -l internal/connector/discord/discord.go internal/connector/discord/discord_test.go` empty (clean). Boundary verified: only internal/connector/discord/discord.go and internal/connector/discord/discord_test.go modified for this round; all 6 BUG-014-002 artifacts created under specs/014-discord-connector/bugs/BUG-014-002-rate-limit-header-no-upper-bound/; parent state.json updated. No spec 055 / cmd/core / internal/api / internal/web / internal/notification / internal/pipeline / config / scripts / smackerel.sh / other-spec-state.json edits staged. No lint-suppression comments introduced. No tests skipped or weakened. Gate G041 honoured — no DoD items reformatted to bypass checkbox accounting; all 7 are - [x] with embedded evidence.

Appended Chaos R30 execution-history entry to specs/014-discord-connector/state.json with BUG-014-002 reference added to resolvedBugs[]; the bug artifact set (spec.md, design.md, scopes.md, report.md, state.json, uservalidation.md) is fully cross-linked. Sweep ledger .specify/memory/sweep-2026-05-23-r30.json round 24 entry updated to completed_owned with findings=4, bugsSpawned=1, bugId=BUG-014-002-rate-limit-header-no-upper-bound, bugFinalStatus=resolved.
